name: Controversial Take Opener
trigger: Flags a divisive opinion to spark debate
template: This may be controversial but [OPINION]
why_it_works: Promising a controversial take primes viewers to stay and judge for themselves.
examples: This may be controversial but ___

name: Everything You Knew Is Wrong
trigger: Claims accepted knowledge is false
template: Everything you knew about [TOPIC] is 100% WRONG! [EXAMPLE]
why_it_works: Challenging the viewer's existing beliefs creates dissonance they need resolved.
examples: Everything you knew about ___ is 100% WRONG! (+ a good example)

name: Bust The Niche Myth
trigger: Calls out a widely believed falsehood
template: Don't believe this [NICHE] myth..
why_it_works: Naming a myth promises to correct a misconception the viewer might hold.
examples: Don't believe this (insert niche) myth..

name: Targeted Stop Scrolling
trigger: Names a specific audience to make them stop
template: [TYPE OF PEOPLE].. stop scrolling!
why_it_works: Directly addressing a group makes the right viewer feel personally called out.
examples: ___ type of people.. stop scrolling!

name: This Is For You
trigger: Self-identification with a behavior or trait
template: If you're like me and do [X], then this one is for you.
why_it_works: Self-recognition makes the viewer feel the content was made just for them.
examples: If you're like me and do X, then this one is for you.
This is for the (type of person) XX looking for (result)
If you have XX, listen up!

name: Exclusive Audience Filter
trigger: Excludes most viewers to heighten relevance
template: This video is only for [X] people, so keep scrolling if that's not you.
why_it_works: Telling people to leave makes the intended audience lean in harder.
examples: This video is only for X people, so keep scrolling if that's not you.

name: Listen Up If You Struggle
trigger: Flags a struggle to grab affected viewers
template: If you have trouble doing [X], you might want to listen up..
why_it_works: Calling out a specific pain point captures exactly the people who feel it.
examples: If you have trouble doing XX, you might want to listen up..
Watch this if you hate feeling XX..

name: Perfect Product Found
trigger: Promises a solution to a known problem
template: I just found the perfect product that helps with [PROBLEM]
why_it_works: Announcing a discovery taps the viewer's hope for an easy fix.
examples: I just found the perfect product that helps with ___

name: Simple Hack Promise
trigger: Offers an easy method for a desired outcome
template: Here's a simple hack to help you do [TASK]
why_it_works: Framing the tip as simple lowers the perceived effort of trying it.
examples: Here's a simple hack to help you do ___
Life Hack: Try [product] for [pain point]..
This is the lazy way to get (insert result)..
This is the easiest tip you can follow to overcome XX..

name: Hack Saves You Money
trigger: Quantifies savings from a niche trick
template: This [NICHE] hack could save you [AMOUNT] amount of money
why_it_works: A concrete money-saving promise gives an immediate reason to keep watching.
examples: This (insert niche) hack could save you XX amount of money

name: Buy This Instead
trigger: Swaps a popular product for a better pick
template: Instead of buying this [POPULAR PRODUCT] I have another recommendation..
why_it_works: Positioning against a known product makes your alternative instantly understood.
examples: Instead of buying this (hold up popular product) I have another recommendation..
The perfect (insert program) doesn't exist.. but this is a close second..

name: Stop Doing This
trigger: Warns viewers off a common mistake
template: If you are into [NICHE] you need to stop doing this..
why_it_works: A warning that you're doing something wrong creates urgency to learn what.
examples: If you are into (insert niche) you need to stop doing this..
This is why your ___ isnt working..
Don't make this mistake when doing ___

name: Red Flags To Watch
trigger: Promises warning signs to avoid
template: Red flags to look for in [NICHE]..
why_it_works: Listing red flags appeals to the fear of being caught out or scammed.
examples: Red flags to look for in (insert niche)..

name: Game Changer Warning
trigger: Frames an announcement as a warning
template: Warning to [NICHE]: this is a game changer.
why_it_works: The word warning interrupts the scroll and signals high stakes.
examples: Warning to (insert niche): this is a game changer.

name: Before You Do This Like Always
trigger: Interrupts a routine before a mistake
template: Before you do [X] like always.. check out [Y]
why_it_works: Pausing an automatic habit makes viewers curious what they've been missing.
examples: Before you do XX like always.. check out XX

name: Reaction You Won't Believe
trigger: Withholds someone's surprising reaction
template: I couldn't believe [PERSON]'s reaction to [X]..
why_it_works: An unseen reaction creates an information gap viewers stay to close.
examples: I couldn't believe (insert person)'s reaction to XX..

name: Can They Do It In Time
trigger: Sets up a timed challenge with unknown outcome
template: Let's see if [PERSON] can do [X] in [TIME]..
why_it_works: A challenge with an uncertain result keeps viewers watching for the payoff.
examples: Let's see if (Insert person) can do XX in (amount of time)..

name: See Differently In 30 Seconds
trigger: Promises a shift in perception soon
template: You will look at [TOPIC] differently after the next 30 seconds.
why_it_works: Promising a perspective change creates anticipation worth waiting for.
examples: You will look at ___ differently after the next 30 seconds.

name: The Only Thing You Need To Know
trigger: Promises one essential piece of knowledge
template: This is the only thing you need to know about [TOPIC]!
why_it_works: Reducing a topic to one must-know point promises high value with low effort.
examples: This is the only thing you need to know about ___!

name: Something That Bothers Me
trigger: Shares a personal frustration
template: Something that's always bothered me about [X]...
why_it_works: A candid personal gripe feels authentic and invites viewers to agree.
examples: Something that's always bothered me about X...

name: I Kept Getting This Problem
trigger: Opens mid-struggle then pivots to a fix
template: I kept getting [PAIN POINT], so I tried this instead!
why_it_works: Dropping into a relatable struggle pulls viewers into the story and its resolution.
examples: I kept getting [pain point], so I tried this instead!

name: I Spent A Week Testing
trigger: Frames effort already invested for the viewer
template: I spent an entire week trying [X].. here's what I discovered..
why_it_works: Showing real time invested promises hard-won findings viewers want shared.
examples: I spent an entire week trying XX.. here's what I discovered..

name: Tested So You Don't Have To
trigger: Did the legwork on the viewer's behalf
template: I tried the top [NICHE] products so you don't have to..
why_it_works: Doing the testing for them saves viewers effort and earns their trust.
examples: I tried the top (insert niche) products so you don't have to..
I tried every affordable XX brand out there and here are the results..

name: Thousands Of Reviews Test
trigger: Cites high review count to validate a test
template: This [PRODUCT TYPE] has over [NUMBER] reviews... let's see if it's worth it
why_it_works: A big review count signals popularity while the test promises an honest verdict.
examples: This [product type] has over 5,000 reviews… let's see if it's worth it

name: Why Conversions Jumped
trigger: Shares a real result from a purchase
template: I bought this [SERVICE] for my business and now my conversions/sales are through the roof!
why_it_works: A concrete success story proves the product works and stirs FOMO.
examples: I bought this (insert service) for my business and now my conversions/sales are through the roof!

name: You Need This Product
trigger: States the viewer must have something
template: You need this [PRODUCT] if you want to save time and effort...
why_it_works: A direct 'you need this' framing asserts value and triggers loss aversion.
examples: You need this (insert product) if you want to save time and effort...
This free tool changed the game for me..

name: Things You're Not Doing
trigger: Counts overlooked actions for improvement
template: [NUMBER] things you're NOT doing to [GOAL]..
why_it_works: A numbered list of gaps promises quick, scannable wins viewers are missing.
examples: 4 things you're NOT doing to improve XX..
3 things you're NOT doing to do XX..

name: Two Signs To Start Or Stop
trigger: Lists signals prompting a behavior change
template: Here are [NUMBER] signs that you should [start/stop] doing [X]
why_it_works: Signs you should act tap self-diagnosis and an easy list format.
examples: Here are 2 signs that you should stop doing XX
Here are 2 signs you should start doing XX

name: Must-Have Products List
trigger: Numbered essentials for a problem
template: [NUMBER] must-have products for people struggling with [PROBLEM]
why_it_works: Pairing a count with a struggle promises curated, relevant solutions.
examples: 6 must-have products for people struggling with (insert problem)

name: One Thing For More Value
trigger: Single action drives an outsized result
template: Get more [VALUE PROP] in your day by doing this one thing..
why_it_works: Reducing the ask to one thing makes the benefit feel effortless to claim.
examples: Get more [value prop] in your day buy doing this one thing..

name: How To Get Result Fast
trigger: Promises an outcome within a tight timeframe
template: How to get [RESULT] in [TIME] or less
why_it_works: Pairing a clear result with a fast timeframe maximizes perceived payoff.
examples: How to get (insert result) in 5 minutes or less
How to impress everyone at the next (insert niche senario)..

name: Why Nobody Talks About This
trigger: Implies a hidden or ignored truth
template: Why is nobody talking about [X] in the [INDUSTRY]?
why_it_works: Framing info as overlooked makes viewers feel they're getting insider knowledge.
examples: Why is nobody talking about XX in the XX industry?
Literally nobody is talking about XX in the (insert niche) world..
Why does no one talk about XX in (insert niche)??

name: The Mistake Everyone Makes
trigger: Names a universal error in the niche
template: This is the mistake every [NICHE] business owner makes..
why_it_works: Claiming everyone makes a mistake makes viewers anxious to check if they do too.
examples: This is the mistake every (insert niche) business owner makes..

name: Skeptical? Try This
trigger: Addresses doubt and offers a test
template: If you're feeling skeptical about [X].. try this..
why_it_works: Acknowledging skepticism disarms doubters and invites them to give it a shot.
examples: If you're feeling skeptical about XX.. try this..
Feeling overwhelmed with XX? Try this next time..

name: Cheaper Than You'd Think
trigger: Reveals a surprisingly low price
template: I can't believe this [HACK] only cost me [PRICE]..
why_it_works: A shockingly low price creates disbelief that viewers stay to verify.
examples: I can't believe this (insert hack) only cost me $10..
This is the best $20 you can spend as a (insert niche)..
This is the best $20 you can spend as a (insert niche)..

name: You Won't Believe This
trigger: Teases something unbelievable
template: You won't believe this thing called [NICHE STEREOTYPE]..
why_it_works: Promising the unbelievable creates a gap viewers must resolve by watching.
examples: You won't believe this thing called "insert niche stereotype"..

name: Biggest Regret Warning
trigger: Shares a regret to spare the viewer
template: My biggest regret as an [PROFESSION] was not discovering this technique sooner - don't make the same mistake I did.
why_it_works: A vulnerable regret framed as a warning makes the advice feel urgent and earned.
examples: My biggest regret as an (insert profession) was not discovering this technique sooner – don't make the same mistake I did.

name: Back In Stock Demand
trigger: Implies high demand and scarcity
template: This is why people keep asking when our [PRODUCT] will be back in stock..
why_it_works: Signaling that others want it and it's sold out triggers scarcity-driven desire.
examples: This is why people keep asking when our XX product will be back in stock..
